bricks.title: Stop building from scratch.
bricks.subtitle: Copy. Paste. Ship.
bricks.description: Production-grade React components designed for builders. Zero dependencies. No 'AI slop'. Just clean, accessible code ready for your next project.

items.question: Why isn't this an npm package?
items.answer: Because you shouldn't be held hostage by a package maintainer. Copy-pasting means the code is yours. Forever. No external dependencies, no security surprises, and zero bloat from code you don't use.

items.question: What makes NachUI different from other libraries?
items.answer: No black boxes. Unlike traditional libraries that hide logic behind abstractions, NachUI gives you the raw source code. It's a complete Design System—tokens, patterns, and guides—that you can audit, modify, and extend as your project evolves.

items.question: Can I use NachUI in commercial projects?
items.answer: Yes. Build anything, ship anywhere. NachUI is free and open-source. No royalties, no attribution required, and no restrictions on how you use the code once it's in your project.

items.question: How do I handle updates?
items.answer: You own the schedule. Copy improvements when you want them, or stick with your customized version. No forced npm updates breaking your production build or introducing unwanted changes.

items.question: Does it work with frameworks like Next.js or Vite?
items.answer: Perfectly. Built with React and Tailwind CSS, it's compatible with any modern environment including Next.js (App & Pages Router), Remix, Vite, Astro, and more.

items.question: How does it work with AI tools like Cursor or Claude?
items.answer: NachUI is AI-native. Since the source code is visible in your repository, AI assistants can read, understand, and modify it directly. It enables seamless collaboration without 'black box' confusion.

items.question: Is it accessible?
items.answer: Always. All components follow WCAG 2.2 and WAI-ARIA standards out of the box. Keyboard navigation, screen reader support, and proper focus states are fundamental, not optional.
